skill.name: knowledge
skill.description: Display project knowledge base status, entry counts, cache statistics, and recent learnings.
skill.summary: Summarize the current project knowledge base in a readable status view.
skill.author: 0xrdan

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command execution and weak cryptography, but the cited lines are Markdown paths, output examples, and plain frontmatter text. The skill is still low risk because it instructs the assistant to read local knowledge files and summarize their contents.

low_findings.title: Local Knowledge File Reads
low_findings.description: The skill directs the assistant to read project knowledge files and display a summary. This is expected behavior for the skill, but users should understand it may expose local knowledge base contents in the assistant response.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-read instructions are explicit and limited to knowledge base files. No write, network, or shell execution behavior is present in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Commands
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged inline Markdown code formatting and fenced output examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines document file names, headers, and sample display text rather than executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited backticks are Markdown syntax around file paths, output examples, and header patterns. I found no executable script block or shell invocation in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Not Evidenced
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at the frontmatter description line. That line only describes the skill purpose and contains no cryptographic function, hash algorithm, or security-sensitive operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain metadata text: a human-readable description. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or any other weak cryptographic use.

content.value_statement: Project knowledge can become hard to track across sessions and files. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a concise status view of stored learnings.
actual_capabilities: Reads the learning mode status from the project knowledge state file.
Counts entries in cache, pattern, quirk, and decision knowledge files.
Extracts the five most recent learning entries from supported learning files.
Displays cache statistics and total insight counts in a formatted summary.
Shows an empty-state message when knowledge files are missing or empty.
Reports related learning commands for users who want to update the knowledge base.
limitations: It depends on the expected knowledge directory and file names.
It does not create, edit, or delete knowledge entries.
It may miss entries that do not use the documented header format.
It does not verify whether stored learnings are still accurate.

use_cases.title: Check Project Memory Before Coding
use_cases.description: Review known patterns, quirks, and decisions before starting implementation work.

use_cases.title: Share Context During Handoff
use_cases.description: Show recent learnings and knowledge counts before another person continues the project.

use_cases.title: Audit Knowledge Base Coverage
use_cases.description: Confirm whether the project is capturing useful learnings across patterns, quirks, and decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Show Status
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check of the current knowledge base.
prompt_templates.prompt: Show the current project knowledge base status.

prompt_templates.title: Review Recent Learnings
prompt_templates.scenario: Inspect the newest stored insights before work starts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Display the knowledge summary and focus on the most recent learnings.

prompt_templates.title: Check Knowledge Health
prompt_templates.scenario: Look for missing or empty knowledge areas.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the project knowledge base and tell me which sections appear empty or stale.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Handoff Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a concise operational summary for another collaborator.
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize the knowledge base status for a handoff, including learning mode, counts, and recent decisions.

output_examples.input: Show the current project knowledge base status.
output_examples.output: A formatted status report showing learning mode, cache counts, learning counts, recent patterns, recent quirks, and recent decisions.

output_examples.input: Review the knowledge base before I start work.
output_examples.output: A concise project memory summary that highlights active learning status and the newest relevant insights.

output_examples.input: Tell me whether the knowledge base is empty.
output_examples.output: An empty-state summary explaining that no entries are available and which learning commands can populate the knowledge base.

best_practices: Run the skill before major coding sessions to restore project context.
Keep knowledge entries dated so recent learnings sort correctly.
Use consistent pattern, quirk, and decision headings for reliable parsing.
anti_patterns: Do not store secrets or credentials in knowledge files.
Do not rely on the summary as a replacement for source review.
Do not expect the skill to update entries or enable learning by itself.

faq.question: Does this skill modify files?
faq.answer: No. The skill describes a read-only workflow that displays knowledge base status and recent entries.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What files does it read?
faq.answer: It reads the project knowledge state file and supported cache, pattern, quirk, and decision files.

faq.question: Can it create new learnings?
faq.answer: No. It points users to related learning commands, but this skill only displays current status.

faq.question: What happens if the knowledge base is empty?
faq.answer: It shows an empty-state summary and explains that learnings can be added through the related learning workflow.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains no network access, shell execution, or file modification instructions.
